mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean drift when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns when either series decouples, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, positive surprises in industrial production indicate resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike concerns, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening cycles, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above 195 force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL remains above 195 for more than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns when payrolls grow but unemployment stagnates, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend levels signals robust aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive drift following above-trend GDP prints, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 110 indicates deteriorating manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive when INDPRO drops below 110, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.
